title: The Electromagnetic Wavefront

features.name: signal-crest
features.desc-on-touch: Along this ridge the chaos of the storm edge transforms into sustained carrier wave. The broadcast you feel here is not metaphorical.

features.name: organized-static
features.desc-on-touch: Static resolves into pattern when you listen at threshold. This pool holds the moment chaos chooses order.

features.name: the-wavefront-edge
features.desc-on-touch: The physical boundary between storm and signal. You can see the transition, a thin line where turbulence becomes radiance.

features.name: interference-cascade
features.desc-on-touch: Layers of frequency falling through themselves. Each layer contradicts the one above.

features.name: measurement-chip
features.desc-on-touch: An old circuit board attempting to quantify the unquantifiable. Its readings drift with every gust.

inscription: where the storm learns its own pattern, the broadcast learns to speak backwards, and the archive stops listening to its own edges

file.name: Storm_Signal_Cascade_44kHz_UnfinishedTransmission.mp3
file.note: a 44-second recording of the wavefront organizing itself; it ends abruptly as if the storm forgot to continue

exits.label: back along the storm's inner eye
where the storm's thought becomes slower, more deliberate
toward the place where signals wash ashore and are sorted
where wavefront becomes dispersed coastal weather
